pages.raw_text:
In order to place before its read
ers the full import of the referen-
dunn measure now before the city
council of Spokane, The Press here
with presents a draft of the pro-
posed ordinance. A close perusal
of it as plan that is commanding
attention all over the United States
will be worth while whether or not
the reader is prepared to approve
it.


Sec. I. That every ordinance
granting, amending or renewing a
franchise for public utility which
shall have passed its third reading
in the city council of said city shall,
before final passage, lie on the
table 80 days. and if within that
time a petition signed by Is per
cent of the registered voters of the
city be filed with the city clerk
asking that such ordinance be SUBJ
mitted to the vote of the qualified
electors of the city, it shall be so
substituted at the next city election
or, at the option of the applicant for
said franchise, at special election
to be called for that purpose, and
If majority of the votes of those
voting on the question shall favor
it's passage it shall then be put up
on its final passage, but not other
wise; provided, that in the event
that said applicant shall exercise
his option of requesting a special
election, then and in that case he
shall at the time of making such
request, deposit with the proper Of
ficer of the city the entire estimat-
ed cost Ito be estimated by the city
clerk Of holding such election, but
in the event of the said question
being submitted at regular city
election it shall be so submitted
without cost to the applicant.


Sec. 2. Every petition to the
city council proposing an instruc-
ton to public official and signed
by at least Is per cent of the regis
tered voters Of the city shall be
received and referred to the com
mittee of the whole council, testi-
mony shall be taken and the meas
ure reported back together with
such amendments, substitute or
